title: Advansed Frontend App - Demo Project
description: This is a full-featured React application developed to showcase modern frontend development skills, DevOps practices, and the complete development cycle from code to production.
tech_stack:
**Core Technologies:**
• React 18.2.0 with TypeScript 4.5.5
• Redux Toolkit 1.9.0 + RTK Query
• React Router DOM 6.2.1
• i18next 21.9.0 for internationalization

**Build & Tools:**
• Webpack 5.74.0 with custom configuration
• Vite 3.2.7 for development server
• Babel with i18next-extract plugins
• ESLint with custom plugin (eslint-plugin-konstantine899-plugin)
• Prettier for formatting
• Stylelint for SCSS
• Husky for pre-commit hooks

**Testing:**
• Jest 27.5.1 with 84 unit tests
• Testing Library (React, DOM, User Event)
• Storybook 6.5.13 for components
• Loki 0.30.3 for visual testing
• Cypress 11.0.0 for E2E tests

**UI Libraries & Animations:**
• @headlessui/react for accessible components
• @react-spring/web for animations
• @use-gesture/react for gestures
• react-device-detect for responsiveness

**API & Data:**
• Axios for HTTP requests
• JSON Server for mock API
• JSON Web Token for authentication
• ts-morph for AST manipulation

**DevOps & Deployment:**
• GitHub Actions for CI/CD
• GitHub Pages for frontend
• Vercel Serverless Functions for API
• gh-pages for manual deployment
architecture:
Project built using **Feature-Sliced Design (FSD)** methodology with clear separation of concerns:

**Architecture Layers:**
• **Entities** - business logic (User, Article, Comment, Profile)
• **Features** - user scenarios (Auth, ArticleRating, ThemeSwitcher)
• **Widgets** - composite components (Page, Sidebar)
• **Pages** - application pages
• **Shared** - reusable components and utilities

**Key Principles:**
• Strict TypeScript typing
• Module and dependency isolation
• Code splitting and lazy loading
• Dependency inversion through abstractions
features:
**Functionality:**
• Full CRUD for articles with validation
• JWT authentication with roles (admin/user)
• Internationalization (Russian/English)
• Dark/light theme with localStorage persistence
• Responsive design for all devices
• Notification system
• Article rating with animations
• Article comments

**Performance:**
• Route-based code splitting
• Lazy loading of components
• Bundle optimization (Tree shaking)
• Request caching via RTK Query

**UX/UI:**
• Accessibility (ARIA attributes)
• Semantic markup
• Smooth animations
• Intuitive navigation
ci_cd:
**Complete GitHub Actions Pipeline:**
• Automatic runs on push/pull request
• TypeScript and SCSS linting
• 84 unit tests with HTML reports
• Webpack and Vite builds
• Visual testing (Loki)
• Automatic deployment to GitHub Pages

**Multi-platform Deployment:**
• **Frontend:** GitHub Pages (https://konstantine899.github.io/advansed-frontend-app/)
• **Backend:** Vercel Serverless Functions (https://advansed-frontend-app-xry5.vercel.app/)
• **Documentation:** Storybook static build

**Code Quality:**
• Pre-commit hooks with linting
• Automatic formatting
• TypeScript type checking
• Test coverage reports
testing:
**Multi-level Testing:**
• **84 unit tests** with Jest
• **Visual testing** with Loki
• **Component testing** with Storybook
• **E2E tests** with Cypress
• **API integration tests**

**Quality Tools:**
• ESLint with 8+ configurations
• Stylelint for SCSS
• Prettier for consistent style
• TypeScript strict mode
• Circular dependency detection
demo:
**Test Accounts:**
• 👑 Administrator: `admin` / `123` (full access)
• 👤 User: `user` / `123` (limited access)

**What to Test:**
1. **CRUD Operations:** Create, read, update, delete articles
2. **Authentication:** Login/logout, protected routes
3. **Internationalization:** Language switching
4. **Themes:** Light/dark theme
5. **Responsiveness:** Different screen sizes
6. **Performance:** Fast loading, lazy loading
